description: The Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan (PDKI) is a Kurdish political party founded in 1945 in Mahabad by [Qazi Muhammad](/people/qazi-muhammad). It played a central role in the short-lived [Republic of Mahabad](/events/republic-of-mahabad-1946) and has been a leading voice for Kurdish autonomy within Iran ever since. After the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979), the party initially participated in the new political process — its leader [Abdul Rahman Ghassemlou](/people/abdul-rahman-ghassemlou) was elected to the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in August 1979 with over 80% of the vote in West Azerbaijan — but the Islamic Republic banned the party, rejected Ghassemlou's credentials, and launched a military campaign against Kurdish regions. Ghassemlou was [assassinated in Vienna in 1989](/events/ghassemlou-assassination-1989), and his successor [Sadegh Sharafkandi](/people/sadegh-sharafkandi) was assassinated in the [Mykonos restaurant attack](/events/mykonos-restaurant-assassination-1992) in Berlin in 1992. The party has operated in exile since the early 1980s and is currently led by [Mustafa Hijri](/people/mustafa-hijri).
editorial_notes: Known by acronym PDKI (or KDPI in Kurdish: Parti Demoqrati Kurdistani Eran). Ghassemlou assassinated by Iranian agents in Vienna 1989. His successor Sadegh Sharafkandi also assassinated in Berlin 1992 (Mykonos restaurant).
